channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: SpaceX mission to replace stuck NASA astronauts launches
item.description: A Falcon 9 rocket carrying the Crew-10 successfully launched from NASA's Kennedy Space Center at 7:03 p.m. ET.
item.dc:creator: Brooke Shafer, WFLA, NBC

item.title: United Airlines gate agent punched in face at Dulles International Airport; passenger arrested
item.description: Police are saying that an airline passenger faces charges after he punched a United Airlines gate agent Thursday night at Washington Dulles International Airport (IAD).
item.dc:creator: Brian Farrell, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump signs order to gut staff at Voice of America and other US-funded media organizations
item.description: President Donald Trump's administration on Saturday began making deep cuts to Voice of America and other government-run, pro-democracy programming, with the organization's director saying all VOA employees have been put on leave.
item.dc:creator: NICHOLAS RICCARDI and DAVID BAUDER, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Trump invokes 18th century law to speed deportations, judge stalls it hours later
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A federal judge on Saturday barred the administration of President Donald Trump from deportations under an 18th century law that Trump invoked just hours earlier asserting the United States was being invaded by a Venezuelan gang and that he had new powers to remove its members from
item.dc:creator: NICHOLAS RICCARDI and WILL WEISSERT, Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump invokes Alien Enemies Act in mass deportation effort
item.description: The president’s move comes hours after a judge temporarily blocked the removal of 5 Venezuelans who the Trump administration says are members of Tren de Aragua.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Monster storm across the US sparks threat of tornadoes and fire, killing at least 16
item.description: The threat of violent tornadoes in parts of the U.S. proved deadly as well as destructive as whipping winds moved east into the Mississippi Valley and Deep South on Saturday, with at least 16 people killed and scores of homes decimated.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump orders strikes on Iran-backed Houthi rebels in Yemen and issues new warning
item.description: WEST PALM BEACH, Fla. (AP) — President Donald Trump said he ordered a series of airstrikes on the Houthi-held areas in Yemen on Saturday, promising to use “overwhelming lethal force” until Iranian-backed Houthi rebels cease their attacks on shipping along a vital maritime corridor.
item.dc:creator: MICHELLE L. PRICE, SAMY MAGDY and LOLITA C. BALDOR, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Bulls title banners removed for season after sustaining damage during Disturbed concert
item.description: All six Bulls championship banners have been removed from the United Center for the rest of the season after sustaining damage during a concert.
item.dc:creator: Andy Koval, KREX 5

item.title: Another state looks to ban this common question drivers are asked every day
item.description: A bill has been introduced into the Colorado legislature that would prohibit officers from asking "Do you know why I pulled you over," and instead tell the driver why they are conducting the traffic stop.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Saurer, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Florida teen tortured, killed by couple after dating app meetup: police
item.description: A Florida man and his domestic partner allegedly killed a 16-year-old girl after luring her to their house on a dating app, beating her and dismembering her body.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The line-swapping hack at Disney, Universal and Six Flags that all parents should know about
item.description: Are some of your children too short, too young, or just too terrified to accompany you on your favorite theme park rides? If so, it doesn't have to slow you down.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, KRON4

item.title: Voters flood town halls with fears of Social Security cuts, putting heat on GOP over Musk and DOGE
item.description: HOLLAND, Mich. (AP) — One after another, callers on a telephone town hall with U.S. Rep. Bill Huizenga pressed the Michigan Republican about possible cuts to Social Security.
item.dc:creator: JOEY CAPPELLETTI, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Big March storm fans wildfires and kills 3 as forecasters fear weekend tornado outbreak
item.description: OKLAHOMA CITY (AP) — A sprawling storm system crossing the U.S. on Friday caused deadly crashes, damaged buildings and fanned more than 100 wildfires in several central states, prompting evacuation orders in some communities.
item.dc:creator: SEAN MURPHY and RUSS BYNUM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: At least 26 dead in massive US storm after Kansas reports 8 fatalities
item.description: At least 26 people have been killed in a monster storm sweeping across much of the U.S. Kansas authorities said eight people died in a highway pileup caused by a dust storm Friday.
item.dc:creator: Sean Murphy, Russ Bynum And Hallie Golden, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Extreme weather expected Saturday gets rarely used 'high risk' designation
item.description: A dynamic storm system threatening to spawn powerful tornadoes and hail as big as baseballs has earned a relatively rare designation from forecasters.
item.dc:creator: Jeff Martin, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Appeals court says Louisiana can carry out the state’s first nitrogen gas execution next week
item.description: BATON ROUGE, La. (AP) — Louisiana's first execution using nitrogen gas is set to move forward as planned next week after a federal appeals court on Friday vacated a preliminary injunction granted by a lower judge. With a March 18 date hastily nearing, attorneys for Jessie Hoffman Jr.
item.dc:creator: SARA CLINE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Most disapprove of Trump's approach to Ukraine, Russia: Survey
item.description: Nearly six in 10 people, 59 percent, said it was “not too" or “not at all likely” that Trump’s approach to the Russia-Ukraine war will bring long-term peace between the two Eastern European countries. Around 41 percent disagreed.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump pledges to 'expose' his enemies in political speech at Justice Department
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump pledged to “expose” his enemies during a norm-breaking political speech Friday at the Justice Department in which he aired a litany of grievances about the criminal cases he faced and vowed retribution for what he described as the “lies and abuses that have o
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER, ALANNA DURKIN RICHER and ZEKE MILLER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: More than 50 universities face federal investigations as part of Trump's anti-DEI campaign
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — More than 50 universities are being investigated for alleged racial discrimination as part of President Donald Trump’s campaign to end diversity, equity and inclusion programs that his officials say exclude white and Asian American students.
item.dc:creator: COLLIN BINKLEY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Oklahoma Ethics Commission votes to pursue prosecution against State Supt. Walters
item.description: On Thursday, the Oklahoma Ethics Commission voted to pursue prosecution against State Superintendent Ryan Walters for alleged campaign violations.
item.dc:creator: Dylan Brown / KFOR, NewsNation

item.title: DHS agents search Columbia dorm rooms after Khalil arrest
item.description: Department of Homeland Security agents searched two dorm rooms at Columbia University Thursday night, the school said, a move that comes less than a week after the federal arrest of former grad student Mahmoud Khalil.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, NewsNation

item.title: Burn pit fund for veterans on chopping block in GOP spending bill
item.description: The fund helps former service members sickened by military toxic exposures, including burn pit smoke and Agent Orange contamination.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, NewsNation

item.title: Wisconsin authorities release details on missing teen's recovery
item.description: Lillian Asala, a 13-year-old from Wisconsin, was reported missing on March 1. Her mother was arrested in connection to her disappearance.
item.dc:creator: Indiana Schilz, NewsNation

item.title: Second judge blocks mass firings of probationary federal workers
item.description: A second federal judge has blocked the Trump administration from firing federal workers, issuing an order Thursday that probationary employees be reinstated weeks after their dismissal.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, KRON4

item.title: Some student loan repayment plans have been suspended: What you should know
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The Trump administration's recent changes to student loans are causing frustration and confusion for some borrowers.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Luigi Mangione's defense fund raises over $722K
item.description: "People are raising money not for his innocence but because they're in support of allegedly what he did.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Vargas, KRON4

item.title: VIDEO: American Airlines plane evacuated in Denver after fire aboard
item.description: Passengers were evacuated Thursday evening from an American Airlines flight after a fire was reported.
item.dc:creator: Heather Willard, Fox 5

item.title: John Feinstein, bestselling author and one of the country's foremost sports writers, dies at 69
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — John Feinstein, one of the country's foremost sports writers and the author of numerous bestselling books, including the groundbreaking “A Season on the Brink” about college basketball coach Bob Knight, died unexpectedly Thursday. Feinstein was 69.
item.dc:creator: HOWARD FENDRICH, Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trader Joe's recalls 61K sparkling water bottles
item.description: Trader Joe's announced a recall of two lots of the popular Gerolsteiner Sparkling Natural Mineral Water, a non-Trader Joe's branded item, which the grocery giant sells.
item.dc:creator: Russell Falcon, ABC 4

item.title: Jewish protesters flood Trump Tower's lobby to demand Mahmoud Khalil's release
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Demonstrators from a Jewish group filled the lobby of Trump Tower on Thursday to denounce the immigration arrest of Mahmoud Khalil, a pro-Palestinian activist who helped lead protests against Israel at Columbia University.
item.dc:creator: CEDAR ATTANASIO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Some universities are freezing hiring and laying off staff as Trump cuts federal funding
item.description: Universities across the U.S. have announced hiring freezes, citing new financial uncertainty as the Trump administration threatens a range of cuts to federal contracts and research grants. Some have announced layoffs.
item.dc:creator: CLAIRE RUSH, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: John Feinstein, one of country's foremost sports writers, dies suddenly
item.description: John Feinstein, one of the country's foremost sports writers and the author of numerous bestselling books, including the groundbreaking “A Season on the Brink” about college basketball coach Bob Knight, died unexpectedly Thursday. Feinstein was 69.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Man claims amnesia after St. Pete police say he rammed couple off road, killing them
item.description: The man accused of killing a woman and injuring her boyfriend faced a judge for the first time on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Georgia McCarthur, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Putin agrees in principle with proposal for Ukraine ceasefire and says more discussions are needed
item.description: Russian President Vladimir Putin said Thursday that he agrees in principle with a U.S. proposal for a 30-day ceasefire in Ukraine, but he emphasized that the terms are yet to be worked out and added that any truce should pave the way to lasting peace.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Judge orders Trump to rehire probationary workers let go in mass firings across multiple agencies
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — A federal judge in San Francisco ordered President Donald Trump's administration to rehire thousands, if not tens of thousands, of probationary workers let go in mass firings across multiple agencies, blasting their tactics Thursday as he slowed the new president's dramatic down
item.dc:creator: JANIE HAR, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Trump threatens Europe with 200 percent tariff on wine, Champagne
item.description: President Donald Trump on Thursday threatened to put a hefty 200 percent tariff on wine and Champagne coming from Europe if the European Union (EU) does not remove a tariff on whisky.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, WFLA, NBC

item.title: 5 risks facing the US economy under Trump
item.description: Warning signs have been flashing and markets have been sliding amid policy uncertainties and some troubling macroeconomic indicators.
item.dc:creator: Tobias Burns, Fox 5
